text.title: api-error-handling Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for api-error-handling. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Every error response must include type (URI), title (short string), and status (HTTP code).
rule.rationale: These three fields are the RFC 7807 minimum. Clients that parse errors programmatically rely on type as the stable identifier.

rule.statement: The type field must be a URI that identifies the error class, not a generic string.
rule.rationale: URI type enables clients to look up documentation and allows error taxonomies to be machine-navigable.

rule.statement: Always include traceId in every error response.
rule.rationale: Without a trace ID, correlating a client-reported error to a server log entry requires time-based guessing, which fails under concurrent load.

rule.statement: Include detail with a human-readable explanation of this specific occurrence.
rule.rationale: title is generic (e.g. "Validation Error"); detail explains what was wrong in this request.

rule.statement: Include instance as the request path URI where the error occurred.
rule.rationale: Allows log systems to group errors by endpoint without parsing log lines.

rule.statement: For 400/422 validation errors, include an errors array with field, code, and message per violation.
rule.rationale: Field-level errors let clients highlight specific form inputs rather than showing a generic top-level message.

rule.statement: Never include stack traces, internal exception messages, or database error details in responses.
rule.rationale: Internal details reveal implementation, schema, and potential attack vectors to adversaries.

rule.statement: Define error codes as string constants grouped by 4xx vs 5xx; never use raw strings inline.
rule.rationale: Centralized constants prevent typos and make all error codes searchable in one place.

rule.statement: Register a global exception handler for ValidationError and a catch-all handler for Exception; log the catch-all server-side before responding.
rule.rationale: Without a catch-all, unhandled exceptions return raw 500 HTML from the framework, not a structured error body.

rule.statement: Error messages must be actionable: name the field, state the constraint, and describe the fix.
rule.rationale: "Invalid input" tells the client nothing; "Email must be in format user@domain.com" tells them exactly what to change.
